name: Tabuyo del Monte — circular by the Embalse de Valtabuyo
name_original: Tabuyo del Monte — circular por el Embalse de Valtabuyo
region_intro: Hiking around Luyego offers diverse landscapes, from challenging mountain ascents to moderate circular trails. The region is characterized by prominent natural features such as Pico Teleno and the Valle de la Valduerna. Hikers can explore areas around the Embalse de Valtabuyo and the historic Monte Irago, which features the legendary "iron cross." This varied terrain provides options for different fitness levels. Best hiking trails around Luyego The most popular hiking route is Pico Teleno from Filiel, a difficult 10.5 miles (16.9 km) trail that takes about 6 hours 40 minutes to complete. This route involves significant elevation gain to reach the summit of Pico Teleno. Another top favourite among local hikers is Valle de la Valduerna desde Destriana — circular por el río Duerna, a difficult 12.9 miles (20.8 km) path. This circular route explores the terrain around the Duerna river. Local hikers also love the Tabuyo del Monte — circular by the Embalse de Valtabuyo, a 9.9 miles (15.9 km) trail leading through the area surrounding the Valtabuyo Reservoir, often completed in about 4 hours 10 minutes. Hiking around Luyego is defined by varied landscapes, including mountain peaks, river valleys, and reservoir paths. The network of routes offers options for different ability levels, from easy walks to challenging climbs. The routes in Luyego are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 3.5 stars from 5 reviews. More than 80 hikers have used komoot to explore Luyego's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What types of hiking trails can I find in Luyego?
A: Luyego offers a diverse range of hiking trails, catering to various fitness levels. You'll find everything from challenging mountain ascents, such as the route to &lt;strong&gt;Pico Teleno from Filiel&lt;/strong&gt;, to moderate circular trails around natural features like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1271912300/tabuyo-del-monte-circular-by-the-embalse-de-valtabuyo" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Embalse de Valtabuyo&lt;/a&gt;, and easier village loops. The region features prominent natural landmarks like Pico Teleno, the Valle de la Valduerna, and the historic Monte Irago.

Q: Are there any easy hiking trails suitable for beginners or casual walkers in Luyego?
A: Yes, Luyego has options for easier walks. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40015219" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Horse Head Door Knocker – El Ganso Village loop from Huerta Cimeras&lt;/a&gt; is an easy 6.96 km trail that takes less than two hours to complete, with minimal elevation gain. In total, there are 3 easy routes available in the area.

Q: What are the more challenging hikes in Luyego?
A: For experienced hikers seeking a challenge, Luyego offers several difficult routes. The ascent of &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1027272010/pico-teleno-from-filiel" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pico Teleno from Filiel&lt;/a&gt; is a demanding 16.9 km trail with over 1000 meters of elevation gain. Another challenging option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1271928204/valle-de-la-valduerna-desde-destriana-circular-por-el-rio-duerna" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Valle de la Valduerna desde Destriana — circular por el río Duerna&lt;/a&gt;, a 20.8 km route exploring the river terrain. There are 3 difficult routes in total.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes in Luyego?
A: Yes, Luyego features several appealing circular routes. A popular moderate option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1271912300/tabuyo-del-monte-circular-by-the-embalse-de-valtabuyo" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tabuyo del Monte — circular by the Embalse de Valtabuyo&lt;/a&gt;, which is about 15.9 km long. Another notable circular trail is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/17541615" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of Foncebadón – Cruz de Ferro (Iron Cross) loop from Rabanal del Camino&lt;/a&gt;, offering scenic views and historical significance.

Q: What natural features or landmarks can I expect to see while hiking in Luyego?
A: Luyego's hiking trails showcase diverse natural beauty and historical landmarks. You can ascend &lt;strong&gt;Pico Teleno&lt;/strong&gt; for panoramic views, explore the scenic &lt;strong&gt;Valle de la Valduerna&lt;/strong&gt;, or hike around the tranquil &lt;strong&gt;Embalse de Valtabuyo&lt;/strong&gt;. A significant draw is &lt;strong&gt;Monte Irago&lt;/strong&gt;, home to the legendary "iron cross," a traditional stop for pilgrims on the Camino Frances. You might also encounter charming villages like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4464846" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Ganso Village&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1000799" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Rabanal del Camino&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What do other hikers say about the trails in Luyego?
A: The hiking trails in Luyego are generally well-regarded by the komoot community, holding an average rating of 3.5 stars from 5 reviews. Hikers often praise the varied landscapes, which include mountain peaks, river valleys, and reservoir paths, providing options for different ability levels.

Q: Are there any historical or cultural points of interest along the hiking routes?
A: Yes, the region is rich in history, particularly along routes that intersect with the Camino Frances. You can visit the legendary "iron cross" on &lt;strong&gt;Monte Irago&lt;/strong&gt;, a site where pilgrims traditionally place stones. Other points of interest include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1000799" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;village and church of Rabanal del Camino&lt;/a&gt;, &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4464846" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Ganso Village&lt;/a&gt;, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4601962" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hermitage of the Vera Cruz&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: How many hiking routes are available in Luyego?
A: There are 10 hiking routes available in Luyego, offering a good selection for various preferences. These include 3 easy, 4 moderate, and 3 difficult trails.

Q: What is the typical duration for hikes in Luyego?
A: Hiking durations in Luyego vary significantly depending on the trail's difficulty and length. For example, an easy route like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40015219" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Horse Head Door Knocker – El Ganso Village loop&lt;/a&gt; can be completed in under 2 hours, while a moderate trail like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1271912300/tabuyo-del-monte-circular-by-the-embalse-de-valtabuyo" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tabuyo del Monte — circular by the Embalse de Valtabuyo&lt;/a&gt; takes around 4 hours 10 minutes. More challenging ascents, such as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1027272010/pico-teleno-from-filiel" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pico Teleno from Filiel&lt;/a&gt;, can take up to 6 hours 40 minutes.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on Luyego's hiking trails?
A: The terrain in Luyego is quite varied. You can expect everything from accessible paths in lower elevations and around reservoirs to significant elevation gains and rugged paths on mountain ascents like Pico Teleno. The region features a mix of mountain peaks, river valleys, and plateaus, offering diverse walking surfaces.

Q: Are there any trails that offer significant viewpoints or panoramic vistas?
A: Yes, the ascent of &lt;strong&gt;Pico Teleno&lt;/strong&gt; from Filiel is known for offering significant views from its summit. Additionally, the uniquely beautiful plateau leading up to Foncebadon, which includes the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/17541615" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of Foncebadón – Cruz de Ferro (Iron Cross) loop&lt;/a&gt;, provides scenic vistas, especially for those interested in the historical and natural beauty of the area.
tips: Large forest roads surrounded by leafy pine trees that grow around the Valtabuyo reservoir. Ideal trails for your outdoor adventures.
The church's stone bell tower can be seen from a distance as you approach the small town of Tabuyo del Monte, a landmark on your journey.

waypoints.name: Church of Nuestra Señora de la Piedad, Tabuyo del Monte
